# Example of Using Conditional Tests in MCP Conformance Tests
# This demonstrates various ways to use condition prefixes to make tests skip when features are not supported.

# Basic condition - only run this command if stdio transport is supported
[stdio] exec mcp-scripttest-server --stdio
[stdio] cmp stderr "MCP server listening on stdio"

# Multiple conditions - only run if both conditions are satisfied
[stdio] [version 2025-03-26] setstdin {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":1,"method":"initialize","params":{"protocolVersion":"2025-03-26","capabilities":{},"clientInfo":{"name":"ConditionalTestClient","version":"1.0.0"}}}
[stdio] [version 2025-03-26] exec cat | mcp-send --stdio
[stdio] [version 2025-03-26] stdout '"result":'

# Negated condition - only run if NOT on Windows
[!windows] echo "Running on a non-Windows platform"

# Conditions with parameters - check for specific environment
[env TEST_MODE conformance] echo "Running in conformance test mode"

# Feature-specific tests - only run if extended tests are enabled
[extended] echo "Running advanced extended tests"
[extended] [tools] echo "Running extended tool tests"

# Version-specific API tests
[version 2024-11-05] setstdin {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":2,"method":"2024_specific_method"}
[version 2024-11-05] exec cat | mcp-send --stdio

[version 2025-03-26] setstdin {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":3,"method":"2025_specific_method"}
[version 2025-03-26] exec cat | mcp-send --stdio

# Platform-specific tests
[windows] echo "Running Windows-specific tests"
[unix] echo "Running Unix-specific tests"
[macos] echo "Running macOS-specific tests"
[linux] echo "Running Linux-specific tests"

# HTTP and SSE tests
[http] exec mcp-scripttest-server --http=localhost:9000
[http] cmp stderr "MCP server listening on http://localhost:9000"

[sse] [http] exec mcp-scripttest-server --http=localhost:9001 --sse
[sse] [http] cmp stderr "MCP server listening on http://localhost:9001 with SSE enabled"

# Optional tools tests
[tools] setstdin {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":4,"method":"tools/list"}
[tools] exec cat | mcp-send --stdio
[tools] stdout '"tools":'

# Combining standard and custom conditions (custom conditions would be defined in your test setup)
[tools] [feature advanced_tools] echo "Advanced tools feature is enabled"

# Using ? prefix to make a command optional regardless of result
? echo "This command always runs and continues even if it fails"

# Using ! prefix to indicate a command should fail
! exec non-existent-command
